purpose: Generated primitive task interface for EG-RSA-V2. This file is derived from a Eureka-like environment-level task file and exposes primitive observation/action variables, safe formula variables/functions, semantic roles, and task text. It does not expose any previous reward schema or previous diagnostic/event definitions.
task_description: Land the lunar module safely between the flags. A good landing should be centered, slow, upright, fuel-efficient, and should end with stable ground contact rather than bouncing or crashing.

environment_source.observation_space_description: 8-dimensional observation containing horizontal position, vertical position, horizontal velocity, vertical velocity, lander angle, angular velocity, left leg contact, and right leg contact.
environment_source.action_space_description: Discrete actions: do nothing, fire left orientation engine, fire main engine, fire right orientation engine.
environment_source.step_semantics_summary: The step function advances lunar lander dynamics and returns observation, environment reward, termination flags, and info. EG-RSA must not use the official reward as feedback for reward editing; it may be recorded only for posthoc reporting.

observation_variables.name: x
observation_variables.description: Horizontal position relative to landing pad center.

observation_variables.name: left_contact
observation_variables.description: Whether the left leg contacts ground.

observation_variables.name: right_contact
observation_variables.description: Whether the right leg contacts ground.

action_variables.name: main_engine
action_variables.description: Main engine action indicator or throttle proxy.

action_variables.name: side_engine
action_variables.description: Side engine action indicator or signed side-thrust proxy.

bootstrap_interface_policy.principle: V2 avoids previous-schema leakage by converting an environment-level task file into an isolated primitive interface. The bootstrap agent only receives primitive observation/action variables, safe formula functions, task text, and output format constraints.
bootstrap_interface_policy.formula_boundary: Generated formulas must use only allowed_formula_variables and allowed_formula_functions.

action_cost_policy_v2.legacy_action_penalty: Do not use for LLM-generated custom formulas; runtime action_penalty is built-in -sum(action^2).
